name: shravan-dev-workflow
description: Codex-first development workflow plugin with spec design swarms, manual grill interviews, docs maintenance, adversarial council review, security scan routing, implementation review orchestration, plan workflows, debugging, skill audits, TUI presentation guidance, and PM Linear work.

interface.shortDescription: Codex-first workflow with spec design, review, security routing, debugging, TUI, and PM
interface.longDescription: Use Shravan Dev Workflow to shape specs with bounded design swarms, manually grill ideas or plans one question at a time, maintain docs and changelog runbooks, adversarially review drafted specs with council lanes, route explicit security audits to the official Codex Security workflows, and run evidence-backed implementation reviews with bounded reviewer lanes, Codex subagents as the default and majority backend, optional Claude or Gemini/agy lanes when requested, and agy as an external model lane for substantial reviews. It also validates and addresses accepted review findings, packages copy-pasteable plan and implementation handoffs, runs plan-review swarms or validate-execute written plans with controller-owned verification, investigates bugs before fixes, audits skills from real session evidence, and includes TUI presentation guidance plus PM Linear work organization.
defaultPrompt: Use spec-design-swarm to shape this design before planning.
Use grill-interview to grill me on this idea one question at a time.
Use maintain-docs to reconcile these docs with current code and plans.
Use spec-adversarial-council-design to attack this spec before implementation.
Use security-scan-router to choose the right Codex Security scan.
Investigate this bug before fixing it.
Audit these skills against session evidence.
